problem: Council decisions are public but too dense for residents to understand quickly.
user: Radu, a neighborhood association member tracking zoning decisions.
key_prompts: Turn this complaint into a 48h civic product brief: residents cannot understand council decisions before they affect a neighborhood.
Extract the main entities, dates, locations, and decision effects from this council decision text. Do not invent missing data.
Create a six-step user flow for a resident who wants to understand one new council decision.
Red-team this prototype for ways it could mislead residents or overstate legal certainty.
Clean this build log without changing facts. Preserve prompt excerpts exactly.
what_ai_generated_well: AI helped turn dense source text into a first-pass summary and spotted missing citation fields.
what_was_wrong_and_corrected_manually: AI invented one affected street name that was not in the source. The team removed it and added source snippets.
what_was_hard: The source PDFs had inconsistent formatting and several scanned pages.
what_works: The demo can paste one decision text and produce a cited resident brief.
what_does_not_work: The demo does not yet process scanned PDFs without manual OCR.
next_steps: Add OCR, test with two neighborhood groups, and ask the city clerk to verify terminology.
